# CGIAR Climate Data Hub — Wikis

> Reference wikis for climate-data methodology in African adaptation work. Written for proposal authors, NAP teams, and CGIAR programme designers who need defensible citations when choosing climate models, datasets, and projections.

These wikis explain what climate models are, how they are selected for African regions, what datasets exist, and how to cite climate-data choices in proposals and reports. All content is anchored to peer-reviewed literature and grey-lit from regional climate bodies (IPCC, WMO, ICPAC, SADC-CSC, AGRHYMET).

The wikis are published by the CGIAR Climate Data Hub and maintained by the Alliance Bioversity-CIAT climate team. They are designed to be citable at a specific version: each page carries a `version` field in its frontmatter, and old versions are retained in git history for citation stability.

Base URL: https://cgiar-climate-data-hub.github.io/wikis/

## Climate data for African adaptation

- [Section overview](https://cgiar-climate-data-hub.github.io/wikis/aaa-atlas/): Purpose, audience, and index of all pages in this section.
- [Climate projections for Africa — a 10-minute primer](https://cgiar-climate-data-hub.github.io/wikis/aaa-atlas/projections-primer/): What a climate projection is (and is not), the three sources of uncertainty (scenario / model / internal variability), how to read a projection chart honestly, and how projections fit into a GCF or Adaptation Fund climate rationale.
- [Baselines and reference periods](https://cgiar-climate-data-hub.github.io/wikis/aaa-atlas/baselines/): The three baselines a reader will encounter — 1850–1900 (IPCC pre-industrial), 1991–2020 (WMO observational), 1995–2014 (CMIP6 / NEX-GDDP reference) — and why different tools use different ones.
- [Climate models 101](https://cgiar-climate-data-hub.github.io/wikis/aaa-atlas/climate-models-101/): What a Global Climate Model (GCM) is, what physics runs inside it, the difference between an ESM and an AOGCM, why ~50 modelling centres each build their own model, what the CMIP framework does, and which 18 models sit behind NASA's NEX-GDDP-CMIP6 product.
- [African CMIP6 Ensembling — the AAA Adaptation Atlas approach](https://cgiar-climate-data-hub.github.io/wikis/aaa-atlas/african-cmip6-ensembling/): Why per-region ensemble tuning matters, the AFR-13 / AFR-8 / FULL-18 ensemble options, the hot-model problem (CanESM5 exclusion), the East African Paradox, and what's coming from CMIP7 and CORDEX-Africa.
- [Recommendations for CGIAR](https://cgiar-climate-data-hub.github.io/wikis/aaa-atlas/cgiar-recommendations/): Prescriptive defaults for CGIAR centres on observed-climate datasets, future-projection datasets, ensembles, and scenarios. Explicit do-nots.
- [CMIP7 and future projections](https://cgiar-climate-data-hub.github.io/wikis/aaa-atlas/future-projections/): What is coming from CMIP7 and CORDEX-Africa, and how the wikis will be updated.
- [Glossary](https://cgiar-climate-data-hub.github.io/wikis/aaa-atlas/glossary/): Definitions of key terms used across this section.
- [References](https://cgiar-climate-data-hub.github.io/wikis/aaa-atlas/references/): Curated bibliography for the section.

## Drafts in progress

The following pages exist as structural stubs — content is being written:

- Why models disagree (`/wikis/aaa-atlas/why-models-disagree/`)
- Downscaling: from global to local (`/wikis/aaa-atlas/downscaling/`)
- Bias correction explained (`/wikis/aaa-atlas/bias-correction/`)
- The dataset landscape (`/wikis/aaa-atlas/dataset-landscape/`)
- Evaluating models for Africa — regional evaluation (`/wikis/aaa-atlas/regional-evaluation/`)
- The East African Paradox (`/wikis/aaa-atlas/east-african-paradox/`)
- Voices from the field (`/wikis/aaa-atlas/voices/`)

## About

- [Contributing a wiki](https://cgiar-climate-data-hub.github.io/wikis/contributing/): How to add or edit a wiki page. Conventions, frontmatter schema, citation style.

Maintainer: Pete Steward, Alliance Bioversity-CIAT (CGIAR) — p.steward@cgiar.org
